comment_text.sn37.32:1.7: AN 6.52:5.2 says a woman insists she be without a co-wife. In Rig Veda 10.159, Sujā performs oblations to gain the power of stripping luster from her co-wives and thus vanquishing them.

root_text.sn37.32:1.4: idaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭhamaṁ ṭhānaṁ dullabhaṁ akatapuññena mātugāmena.
root_text.sn37.32:1.5: Patirūpe kule jāyitvā patirūpaṁ kulaṁ gaccheyyanti—
root_text.sn37.32:1.6: idaṁ, bhikkhave, dutiyaṁ ṭhānaṁ dullabhaṁ akatapuññena mātugāmena.
root_text.sn37.32:1.7: Patirūpe kule jāyitvā, patirūpaṁ kulaṁ gantvā, asapatti agāraṁ ajjhāvaseyyanti—
root_text.sn37.32:1.8: idaṁ, bhikkhave, tatiyaṁ ṭhānaṁ dullabhaṁ akatapuññena mātugāmena.
root_text.sn37.32:1.9: Patirūpe kule jāyitvā, patirūpaṁ kulaṁ gantvā, asapatti agāraṁ ajjhāvasantī puttavatī assanti—
root_text.sn37.32:1.10: idaṁ, bhikkhave, catutthaṁ ṭhānaṁ dullabhaṁ akatapuññena mātugāmena.
root_text.sn37.32:1.11: Patirūpe kule jāyitvā, patirūpaṁ kulaṁ gantvā, asapatti agāraṁ ajjhāvasantī puttavatī samānā sāmikaṁ abhibhuyya vatteyyanti—
root_text.sn37.32:1.12: idaṁ, bhikkhave, pañcamaṁ ṭhānaṁ dullabhaṁ akatapuññena mātugāmena.
root_text.sn37.32:1.13: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, pañca ṭhānāni dullabhāni akatapuññena mātugāmenāti.
root_text.sn37.32:2.1: Pañcimāni, bhikkhave, ṭhānāni sulabhāni katapuññena mātugāmena.
root_text.sn37.32:2.4: idaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭhamaṁ ṭhānaṁ sulabhaṁ katapuññena mātugāmena.
root_text.sn37.32:2.5: Patirūpe kule jāyitvā patirūpaṁ kulaṁ gaccheyyanti—
root_text.sn37.32:2.6: idaṁ, bhikkhave, dutiyaṁ ṭhānaṁ sulabhaṁ katapuññena mātugāmena.
root_text.sn37.32:2.7: Patirūpe kule jāyitvā patirūpaṁ kulaṁ gantvā asapatti agāraṁ ajjhāvaseyyanti—
root_text.sn37.32:2.8: idaṁ, bhikkhave, tatiyaṁ ṭhānaṁ sulabhaṁ katapuññena mātugāmena.
root_text.sn37.32:2.9: Patirūpe kule jāyitvā patirūpaṁ kulaṁ gantvā asapatti agāraṁ ajjhāvasantī puttavatī assanti—
root_text.sn37.32:2.10: idaṁ, bhikkhave, catutthaṁ ṭhānaṁ sulabhaṁ katapuññena mātugāmena.
root_text.sn37.32:2.11: Patirūpe kule jāyitvā patirūpaṁ kulaṁ gantvā asapatti agāraṁ ajjhāvasantī puttavatī samānā sāmikaṁ abhibhuyya vatteyyanti—
root_text.sn37.32:2.12: idaṁ, bhikkhave, pañcamaṁ ṭhānaṁ sulabhaṁ katapuññena mātugāmena.
root_text.sn37.32:2.13: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, pañca ṭhānāni sulabhāni katapuññena mātugāmenā”ti.

translation_text.sn37.32:1.1: “Mendicants, there are five things that are hard to get for ladies who have not made merit.
translation_text.sn37.32:1.3: ‘May I be born into a suitable family!’
translation_text.sn37.32:1.5: ‘Having been born in a suitable family, may I marry into a suitable family!’
translation_text.sn37.32:1.7: ‘Having been born in a suitable family and married into a suitable family, may I live at home without a co-wife!’
translation_text.sn37.32:1.9: ‘Having been born in a suitable family, and married into a suitable family, and living at home without a co-wife, may I have children!’
translation_text.sn37.32:1.11: ‘Having been born in a suitable family, and married into a suitable family, and living at home without a co-wife, and having had children, may I live having mastered my husband!’
translation_text.sn37.32:1.13: These are the five things that are hard to get for ladies who have not made merit.
translation_text.sn37.32:2.1: There are five things that are easy to get for ladies who have made merit.
translation_text.sn37.32:2.3: ‘May I be born into a suitable family!’
translation_text.sn37.32:2.5: ‘Having been born into a suitable family, may I marry into a suitable family!’
translation_text.sn37.32:2.7: ‘Having been born into a suitable family and married into a suitable family, may I live at home without a co-wife!’
translation_text.sn37.32:2.9: ‘Having been born into a suitable family, and married into a suitable family, and living at home without a co-wife, may I have children!’
translation_text.sn37.32:2.11: ‘Having been born into a suitable family, and married into a suitable family, and living at home without a co-wife, and having had children, may I live having mastered my husband!’
translation_text.sn37.32:2.13: These are the five things that are easy to get for ladies who have made merit.”
